content:
## Intake Checklist
A debit-card or electronic-fund-transfer dispute may be filed only when all intake conditions in transaction_exists, transaction_status_is_posted, user_is_verified, account_status_is_open, account_has_no_holds, card_linked_to_account, notice_is_within_statement_window, required_fields_present, dispute_category_is_valid, transaction_type_is_valid, pin_compromised_is_valid, disputed_amount_is_valid, open_dispute_limit_not_exceeded, merchant_contact_requirement_satisfied, transaction_not_already_disputed are satisfied. Confirm the customer is verified before collecting case details, then match the transaction to the customer, account, and card relationship. The transaction must be posted, the account must be open and unrestricted, and the same transaction must not already have an active dispute.

## Required Case Details
Before any state-changing step, collect the complete argument set: transaction_id, account_id, card_id, user_id, dispute_category, transaction_date, discovery_date, disputed_amount, transaction_type, card_in_possession, pin_compromised, contacted_merchant, police_report_filed, written_statement_provided, provisional_credit_eligible, customer_max_liability_amount, card_action. Use the customer's own dates for the transaction and discovery fields, and confirm whether merchant contact is required and satisfied for the reported issue. If any required item is missing, follow ask_user_for_missing_information rather than filing a partial dispute.

## When Intake Fails
If the transaction is still pending, use explain_pending_transaction_not_disputable_yet. If the account is not open, use explain_account_not_eligible_for_debit_dispute; if the account is restricted, use escalate_restricted_account. For duplicate disputes use explain_existing_dispute. For classification, amount, timing, merchant-contact, or open-dispute-limit failures, apply ask_user_to_reclassify_issue, ask_user_for_valid_disputed_amount, deny_not_timely_or_escalate, ask_user_to_contact_merchant_first, or explain_open_dispute_limit as applicable. See "Debit Card Error Notice And Liability" and "Debit Card Provisional Credit Policy" for related intake decisions.
title: Internal: Debit Card Dispute Intake Requirements
